title: Aotearoa Data Explorer | Stats NZ
content:
## Aotearoa Data Explorer
29 September 2025, 12:00pm
This page has information and updates about Aotearoa Data Explorer, what data you can find on it, and training material to help you use the tool.
Aotearoa Data Explorer (ADE) is a free online tool that presents data in a searchable, flexible, and dynamic way. It is an interactive web browser interface where you can view, query, and download data.
Go to Aotearoa Data Explorer News about Aotearoa Data Explorer How to use Aotearoa Data Explorer Data you can find on Aotearoa Data Explorer

## News about Aotearoa Data Explorer

#### Keeping track of data changes on Aotearoa Data Explorer
Data is updated in Aotearoa Data Explorer in accordance with the Release calendar .
Aotearoa Data Explorer changes by date details additional changes such as revisions, corrections, and new series.

#### Adding more data to Aotearoa Data Explorer
We are working to move data currently in Infoshare or only published in Excel/CSV files into Aotearoa Data Explorer. We are changing our data tools has more detail about this work.
We’re inviting expert users to help shape the future of Stats NZ’s data tools:
- Join the Replace Infoshare Test Group and be part of the transformation by emailing datatools@stats.govt.nz .
- Share your feedback through the contact form in ADE or our Info Centre Team.

#### Aotearoa Data Explorer upgrade
Aotearoa Data Explorer (ADE) was upgraded on 29 September 2025.
The upgrade introduced improvements to support New Zealand’s data system and make your experience with ADE better.
Based on your feedback and our commitment to improving Stats NZ’s digital tools, the ADE upgrade included:
- Bug fixes: Items will no longer automatically select or deselect when passing the mouse over. These will now only react to user selection. In downloads, labels will no longer be missing for the last dimension.
- Items will no longer automatically select or deselect when passing the mouse over. These will now only react to user selection.
- In downloads, labels will no longer be missing for the last dimension.
- Improvements: The search function has been refined, including: matches for partial words, no longer macron sensitive, for example, searching for Maori will return results for Māori. Referential metadata can now be downloaded from the Data Download menu. A new option in the Layout screen to separate numeric and non-numeric values (e.g. a “Not available” symbol) into different rows/columns. Visual changes to the filters and advanced selection mode screen.
- The search function has been refined, including: matches for partial words, no longer macron sensitive, for example, searching for Maori will return results for Māori.
- matches for partial words,
- no longer macron sensitive, for example, searching for Maori will return results for Māori.
- Referential metadata can now be downloaded from the Data Download menu.
- A new option in the Layout screen to separate numeric and non-numeric values (e.g. a “Not available” symbol) into different rows/columns.
- Visual changes to the filters and advanced selection mode screen.
- New and updated materials: User guide refreshed to reflect the updates. We’ve heard you prefer shorter guides, so we’ve added a short starter guide to help you get started quickly, alongside the full guide.
- User guide refreshed to reflect the updates.
- We’ve heard you prefer shorter guides, so we’ve added a short starter guide to help you get started quickly, alongside the full guide.
We are improving our digital products and practices to make data more accessible and easier to use for everyone. From now on, we’ll be able to roll out new features that benefit Aotearoa’s data system more frequently. This update to ADE is a key part of our journey to replace Infoshare, which we have just started working on.

#### Stay informed about Aotearoa Data Explorer
Subscribe to newsletters lets you sign up to the Expert Data Users newsletter to stay informed about changes to Aotearoa Data Explorer and other Stats NZ products.

## How to use Aotearoa Data Explorer
Please see below for short videos and user guides to help you use Aotearoa Data Explorer.
Watch our Aotearoa Data Explorer tutorial videos on YouTube to get a basic overview of how to search, customise, and download tables. The link takes you to a play list of short videos on different topics.
View the Quick start guide to Aotearoa Data Explorer to learn how to get started using the tool.
View the Aotearoa Data Explorer user guide for detailed information about using the tool.
View the Aotearoa Data Explorer – API user guide for detailed information about using an Application Programming Interface (API) to retrieve data from Aotearoa Data Explorer.
View the following for software-specific guidance about setting up API requests:
- Using the Aotearoa Data Explorer API in ArcGIS Online
- Using the Aotearoa Data Explorer API in Excel
- Using the Aotearoa Data Explorer API in Power BI
- Using the Aotearoa Data Explorer API in R

#### Start using Aotearoa Data Explorer
Get started with Aotearoa Data Explorer . The direct URL for the data tool is: explore.data.stats.govt.nz.

## Data you can find on ADE
Data you can find on Aotearoa Data Explorer includes:
- business demography,
- household expenditure,
- iwi estimates (2018),
- linked employer-employee data (LEED),
- population estimates,
- population projections, and

#### Data elsewhere on the Stats NZ website
Stats NZ also publishes data on the Infoshare tool and in Excel or CSV files on our website, www.stats.govt.nz/publications . We plan to move this data onto Aotearoa Data Explorer in future.

#### Data from the previous NZ.Stat tool
NZ.Stat was disconnected on 13 September 2024, Aotearoa Data Explorer has now replaced it.
We have created a dataset mapping sheet to help users of the previous NZ.Stat tool find data sets in ADE that have been migrated. If you require more help with locating data on ADE please see our user guide or contact our Information Centre .
If you've got a question about our data tools, contact our Information Centre.
Phone: 0508 525 525 (toll free) +64 4 931 4600
Email: info@stats.govt.nz .
Use our online contact form .
